title: cicer incanum
intro: Cicer incanum is a perennial legume native to the Indian subcontinent, commonly known as the 'wild chickling pea' or 'hoary chickling'. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cicer incanum include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions. Cicer incanum is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or infusion for its herbal uses, and it is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine.
